title: Locus Story Contract
description: Machine-readable user story contract. A structured format for writing user stories that AI coding assistants, CI pipelines, and audit tools can read alongside humans.

$schema.description: Optional. URL to this JSON Schema, for editor tooling.

schema_version.description: Schema version. Semver string. Readers must check major version; handle unknown minor/patch gracefully.

exported_at.description: Optional. ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when this file was exported from the Locus platform. Present when the file was generated by the API or CLI export; absent when hand-authored.

stories.description: All stories for this project. May be empty.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Project metadata. Stable across renames — use `id` as the canonical reference.

id.description: Stable UUID. Never changes. Optional in hand-authored files.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp. When the project was created. Optional in hand-authored files.

Story.title: Story
Story.description: A single user story. Describes what a user can do or see — implementation-agnostic, observable, testable.

id.description: Stable UUID. Never changes, even if title or story_id changes. Use this as the canonical reference in PRs, audit logs, and integrations. Optional in hand-authored files.

story_id.description: Human-readable identifier for the story. Examples: 'US-01', 'AUTH-07', 'PAY-12'. MUST be unique within the file and MUST NOT change once assigned — stories are referenced by story_id in depends_on arrays, PR messages, CI output, and test annotations. Recommended format: <PREFIX>-<NUMBER> (2–8 uppercase ASCII letters, hyphen, zero-padded integer). If the stable UUID id field is present, use that for persistent programmatic references; story_id is for human-readable references.
story_id.x-locus-recommended-pattern-note: Validators SHOULD warn (not error) when story_id does not match this pattern. The pattern is advisory — other formats are not invalid, but tools may surface a lint warning.

title.description: Short, active-voice description of what the user can do. Should begin with '[Actor] can [action]' or similar.
examples: Employee can submit a leave request
Manager can approve or reject a leave request

description.description: Observable behaviour. What a user can do or see. Implementation-agnostic. Specific enough that a QA engineer can verify it without asking the author.

section.description: Feature area grouping for display and filtering. E.g. 'Auth', 'Dashboard', 'Leave Requests'.

status.description: Implementation status. Set by Locus's audit process or manually. Do not infer from other fields.

assignee.description: Username or email of the person responsible for implementing this story. Null if unassigned.

reviewer.description: Username or email of the person reviewing this story's implementation. Null if unassigned.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp. When the story was created.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp. When the story was last modified.

version.description: Monotonically increasing version counter. Starts at 1. Increments on every change.

pr_refs.description: GitHub PR URLs that implement this story. Optional. Strings only in files — rich PRRef objects are API-only. See design decision A.3.

oneOf.description: Linked Jira issue key, e.g. 'PROJ-123'.

jira_key.description: Linked Jira issue key. Null if no Jira link.

oneOf.description: Human-readable, testable conditions that define when this story is done. Not Gherkin/BDD — plain English. Each item should be verifiable by a QA engineer.
examples: Error message appears within 2s if email is invalid
Form state preserved if validation fails

acceptance_criteria.description: Testable acceptance conditions. Null or absent if not yet defined. Enables precise Story Guard scope checking and deterministic audit validation.

oneOf.description: List of story_id values that must be implemented before this story can be started.

depends_on.description: Story IDs that must be implemented before this story. Use story_id values (e.g. 'US-03'), not UUIDs. Null or absent if no dependencies.

oneOf.description: URL to the Figma frame, mockup, design token set, or other design artefact for this story.

design_ref.description: Link to the design reference for this story. Null if no design artefact exists.

oneOf.description: Explicit test file paths (optionally with line anchors) that verify this story. Makes audit deterministic — the audit checks these files rather than inferring from code search.

test_refs.description: Test file references for this story. Null or absent if not yet specified.

compliance.description: Compliance sign-off block. Include for stories requiring formal approval before implementation. Null or absent if not applicable.

oneOf.description: Freetext field for per-story rationale: design decisions, scope exclusions, ADR links, implementation constraints. Distinct from description (observable behaviour) and acceptance_criteria (testable contract).

notes.description: Author notes, rationale, or constraints. Not the observable behaviour (that's description). Null or absent if unused.

oneOf.description: Free-form labels for cross-cutting concerns, ad-hoc grouping, and tooling filters. Examples: 'mobile', 'perf', 'a11y', 'mvp', 'security', 'wave-2'. Each tag is a lowercase alphanumeric string; hyphens allowed; no spaces.

tags.description: Free-form labels. Lowercase, hyphens allowed. Use for cross-cutting concerns not captured by section or priority. Max 20 tags. Null or absent if unused.

oneOf.description: The user role or actor this story is written for. Short identifier — matches the subject of the story title. E.g. 'employee', 'admin', 'guest', 'api-consumer'. Distinct from assignee (implementer) — this is who benefits from the story.

persona.description: The user persona this story is written for. Who benefits from this story. Single value — if a story serves multiple personas it should be split. Null or absent if not applicable (e.g. system-level stories).

risk.description: Risk assessment for this story. Include when the story touches security, data, or regulated behaviour, or when the implementation risk is non-trivial. Null or absent if no specific risk identified.

non_functional.description: Non-functional requirements (NFRs) that apply specifically to this story. Use for story-level quality constraints: latency targets, data retention, availability SLOs, accessibility level. Project-wide NFRs should live in project-level documentation, not on every story. Null or absent if no story-specific NFRs.

Risk.title: Risk
Risk.description: Risk assessment for a story. Captures the level, category, and mitigation plan for stories with non-trivial implementation risk.

level.description: Risk level. critical=blocks ship; high=needs explicit mitigation; medium=needs monitoring; low=noted but no action required.

oneOf.description: Risk category. Use a canonical value where applicable; freeform text is also valid.

category.description: Risk category. Null if not categorised.

oneOf.description: What could go wrong. Specific and actionable. E.g. 'Rate-limiting on payment provider API may cause silent failures during peak load.'

description.description: Human-readable description of the risk. Optional but strongly recommended for high/critical.

oneOf.description: What is being done to reduce or eliminate the risk. E.g. 'Add retry with exponential backoff + dead-letter queue. Alert on DLQ depth > 10.'

mitigation.description: Mitigation plan or action taken. Required when level is critical or high. Optional for medium/low.

oneOf.description: URL to a threat model, security review, architecture decision, or related document.

review_ref.description: Link to supporting risk documentation. Optional.

NonFunctional.title: NonFunctional
NonFunctional.description: Story-level non-functional requirements. Constraints on how the story must be implemented — not what it does (that's the description + acceptance_criteria), but how fast, how reliably, how accessibly. Only include constraints that are specific to this story; project-wide defaults should live elsewhere.

performance.description: Latency, throughput, or resource constraints for this story.

oneOf.description: Availability target expressed as a percentage or SLO string. E.g. '99.9%', '99.95% over 30d rolling window', '4h planned maintenance window per month allowed'.

availability.description: Availability SLO for the feature area this story covers. Null if using project default.

oneOf.description: Accessibility conformance target. Use WCAG level notation or a custom constraint.

accessibility.description: WCAG accessibility conformance level required for this story. Null if using project default or not applicable.

oneOf.description: Data retention constraint for data created or modified by this story. ISO 8601 duration string or human-readable policy. E.g. 'P7Y' (7 years), '90 days', 'delete on account closure'.

data_retention.description: How long data created by this story must be retained. Null if no story-specific constraint.

oneOf.description: Security requirements specific to this story's implementation. E.g. 'All PII fields must be encrypted at rest (AES-256)', 'Tokens must be invalidated on logout', 'Rate-limit to 10 req/min per IP'.

security.description: Security constraints beyond what the compliance block captures. Freeform. Null if no story-specific security NFR.

oneOf.description: Any additional non-functional constraints not captured by the named fields above.

notes.description: Additional NFR notes not captured by named fields. Null if unused.

PerformanceNFR.title: PerformanceNFR
PerformanceNFR.description: Latency, throughput, or resource usage constraints for a story.

max_ms.description: Hard maximum latency in milliseconds. Requests exceeding this are considered failures.

throughput_rps.description: Required sustained throughput in requests per second.

oneOf.description: Freeform performance budget note. Use for constraints not expressible as latency percentiles. E.g. 'Bundle size must not exceed 50KB gzip', 'Initial load must not exceed 2s on 4G'.

Compliance.title: Compliance
Compliance.description: Formal approval record for regulated-sector stories. Include when a human must sign off before implementation (PSD2/3, HIPAA, ISO 27001, SOC 2, or similar).

approved_by.description: Email or username of the approver (CTO, compliance officer, auditor).

approved_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when approval was granted.

oneOf.description: URL to the review issue, PR, or document where approval is recorded.

review_ref.description: Link to the approval record. Optional but recommended for audit trails.

oneOf.description: Free text reason for the compliance block, e.g. regulatory article reference.

audit_note.description: Explanation of why this story requires compliance sign-off. Optional.

$comment: Canonical URL: https://locus.dev/schema/v1.2.json — GitHub raw fallback: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jonybur/locus-story-schema/main/schema/v1.2/stories.schema.json
